documentation.readme: The Offensive Web Application Penetration Testing Framework.
usage_sections: A complete versatile framework to cover up everything from Reconnaissance to Vulnerability Analysis.
Has 5 main phases, subdivided into 14 sub-phases consisting a total of 108 modules.
Reconnaissance Phase has 50 modules of its own (including active and passive recon, information disclosure modules).
Scanning & Enumeration Phase has got 16 modules (including port scans, WAF analysis, etc)
Vulnerability Analysis Phase has 37 modules (including most common vulnerabilities in action).

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

examples: info      Show description of current module.     M
list      List all modules of a category.         :

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with rapid7/metasploit-framework (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with MobSF/Mobile-Security-Framework-MobSF (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with 0xinfection/xsser (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: The Offensive Web Application Penetration Testing Framework.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: The Offensive Web Application Penetration Testing Framework.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/0xinfection/tidos-framework.git; pip3 install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo python3 tidconsole.py  #console interface`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd tidos-framework/core/docker`; `docker build -t tidos .`. The Offensive Web Application Penetration Testing Framework.

name: TIDoS-Framework
summary: The Offensive Web Application Penetration Testing Framework.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.
